message:
feat(vdbe): collation-aware UNIQUE index probe via find_conflicting_rowid_in_index_collated

Direct follow-up to the earlier "vdbe: collation-aware UNIQUE
constraints in MemDatabase + preformatted Insert record path" commit
in this same batch.  That commit handled MemDatabase-side enforcement
and the preformatted record plumbing; this commit extends the same
collation-awareness to the *index-backed* UNIQUE path used by disk-
backed (StorageCursor) tables.

Problem: the old IndexInsert unique-probe called
sc.cursor.index_insert_unique(cx, key_bytes, n_idx_cols, columns_label).
That routine compares index entries bytewise, which is correct for
BINARY collation but silently fails to detect conflicts under
NOCASE / RTRIM / user-defined collations.  Result: after the earlier
core / MemDatabase patches landed, a table with a NOCASE-UNIQUE
column stored in the memdb side would reject a duplicate, but the
exact same schema backed by an on-disk index would accept it.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs  (~186 line delta)

=== Fast path for the common BINARY case ===

- Look up the index root page for the current cursor_id via
  self.cursor_root_pages.get(&cursor_id).copied().
- Resolve the per-column descending flags and per-column collation
  metadata for that root page using the existing helpers
  self.index_desc_flags_for_root(root_page) and
  self.index_collations_for_root(root_page), defaulting to empty
  vecs when the root isn't known.
- Compute uses_collated_unique_probe as
      is_unique && n_idx_cols > 0
          && index_collations.take(n_idx_cols)
             .any(|c| c.as_deref().is_some_and(|n| !n.eq_ignore_ascii_case("BINARY")))
  so the old bytewise fast path is preserved for indexes that
  either have no explicit collation or explicitly declare BINARY
  on every key column.
- When the fast path doesn't apply, snapshot the shared
  collation registry once with
  self.collation_registry.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()).clone()
  so the slow path can compare values without re-locking per row.

=== Slow path: walk the index and compare via compare_index_prefix_keys ===

- New helper fn find_conflicting_rowid_in_index_collated(sc,
  key_bytes, n_idx_cols, desc_flags, collations,
  collation_registry) -> Result<Option<i64>>.
- Parses the incoming key_bytes via parse_record() and bails out
  returning Ok(None) if any of the first n_idx_cols values is
  NULL (SQLite "NULL is distinct in UNIQUE" rule).
- Walks the cursor from first() to last(), materializing each
  existing entry with parse_record() and comparing the prefix to
  the target using compare_index_prefix_keys(existing, target,
  n_idx_cols, desc_flags, collations, collation_registry).  This
  reuses the same comparator the rest of the index machinery uses
  so there's no risk of the unique check disagreeing with the
  sort order.
- On the first Ordering::Equal match, extracts the conflicting
  rowid from the last value of the existing entry
  (SqliteValue::Integer) and returns Some(rowid), falling back to
  sc.cursor.rowid(&sc.cx) if the trailing value isn't an Integer.

=== Wire the new path into the IndexInsert opcode handler ===

- unique_insert_result is now a
  Result<(), (FrankenError, Option<i64>)> where the Option<i64>
  carries the conflicting rowid when the slow path found it.
- When uses_collated_unique_probe is true:
    call find_conflicting_rowid_in_index_collated(...);
    if it returns Some(rowid), short-circuit with a
      UniqueViolation error carrying that rowid as the second
      tuple element so the OE_REPLACE (5) and OE_ABORT (8) branches
      below can find the target row without re-walking the index;
    otherwise, fall through to sc.cursor.index_insert(&sc.cx,
      key_bytes) and return Ok(()).
- When uses_collated_unique_probe is false:
    keep the existing sc.cursor.index_insert_unique() path, but
    wrap the outcome in the new 2-tuple shape so the rest of the
    match arm stays uniform:
        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
        Err(UniqueViolation { columns }) => Err((UniqueViolation { columns }, None)),
        Err(other) => return Err(other),
- The OE_REPLACE (5) / OE_ABORT (8) arms of the conflict handler
  reuse the pre-computed `conflict_rowid` when
  uses_collated_unique_probe is true, or fall back to the
  existing byte-based find_conflicting_rowid_in_index(...) lookup
  when the fast path applied.
- The generic `Err(e) => return Err(e)` arm becomes
  `Err((error, _)) => return Err(error)` to match the new tuple
  shape.

End result: after this commit, disk-backed tables with NOCASE,
RTRIM, or user-defined collation UNIQUE indexes correctly reject
duplicates, and ON CONFLICT REPLACE / ABORT still find the right
row to unwind.  Existing BINARY-UNIQUE workloads stay on the old
fast path and see no performance impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
